title: Gymnogramma cheilanthoides
intro: Gymnogramma cheilanthoides, also known as the silver spleenwort or Gymnogramma fern, is a species of fern in the family Pteridaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects, making it useful for treating various conditions such as wounds, skin infections, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Gymnogramma cheilanthoides include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Gymnogramma cheilanthoides is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is used to treat a range of conditions, including respiratory infections, digestive issues, and skin problems.
taxonomy: Gymnogramma cheilanthoides is classified as a species within the family Asteraceae. This classification places it in the order Asterales. The family Asteraceae is a large group of flowering plants, commonly known as daisies or sunflowers.
